content:
When a new version supersedes an old one, do not erase the older artifact. Instead:

Create a new artifact with an incremented VERSION field.
Include in its SEAL an explicit “supersedes” field with references to prior versions.
Update manifest indexes to mark older versions as superseded, not deleted.

4.3 Text-Based Index Format

For every major subdirectory, maintain a manifest file, for example:

$ARCHIVE_ROOT/meta/index_worlds.tsv
$ARCHIVE_ROOT/meta/index_sessions.tsv
$ARCHIVE_ROOT/meta/index_characters.tsv

A simple line-based format is sufficient. For example, a tab-separated line:

idnamelayerdomaintypeworld_idtimestampversionpathtags

Example entry:

WORLD-MYTH-SPEC-CORE-0100-v0_2Core Mythos SpecWORLDMYTHSPECWORLD-0100202511230015v0_2$ARCHIVE_ROOT/worlds/world-0100/core-spec-v0_2.mdmyth,seed,primary

Future AIs are instructed to:

Read manifest files first when orienting to a directory.
